title: asphodelus albus var. littoralis
intro: Asphodelus albus var. littoralis, also known as the Sea Asphodel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family and is native to the coastal regions of the Mediterranean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Asphodelus albus var. littoralis include reducing inflammation, treating wounds and skin conditions, and potentially lowering cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it has also been used in traditional folk remedies for various ailments.
